headline: Data privacy complaints surge 67% after PDPA amendment
context: Following the 2024 PDPA amendment introducing mandatory breach notification, data privacy complaints to JPDP surged 67% in the second half of 2024.

cards.big: Data privacy complaints rose 67% to 798 after 2024 PDPA amendment took effect
cards.sub: Mandatory breach notification exposed previously hidden violations

cards.big: PDPA 2024 amendment requires breach notification within 72 hours
cards.sub: Penalties increased to RM1 million maximum fine for non-compliance

cards.big: Right to data protection not explicitly in Federal Constitution
cards.sub: PDPA remains the sole statutory protection for personal data

cards.big: 56% of complaints involved financial services and telecommunications sectors
cards.sub: Consumers increasingly aware of data rights but lack enforcement support

cards.big: 67% surge shows violations were always happening, just unreported before
JPDP staffing remains at 85 officers for 798 complaints and growing caseload
